document.source_title: PCOS gets a new name: Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome, to aid faster diagnosis, treatment

claims.claim_text: PCOS is being renamed to PMOS to improve diagnosis and treatment.
claims.evidence_text: The Endocrine Society...has decided to rename Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) as Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS).

entities.name: Endocrine Society
entities.description: A global organisation of scientists and physicians devoted to hormone-related conditions.
entities.evidence_text: The Endocrine Society, a global organisation of scientists and physicians devoted to hormone-related conditions...

entities.name: Helena J. Teede
entities.description: Author of the paper and director of Monash University’s Monash Centre for Health Research & Implementation.
entities.evidence_text: Prof. Helena Teede, author of the paper, director of Monash University’s Monash Centre for Health Research & Implementation...

events.event: Renaming of PCOS to PMOS announced
events.evidence_text: The Endocrine Society...has decided to rename Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) as Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS).

metrics.name: Estimated number of women affected by PCOS
metrics.evidence_text: PCOS is estimated to affect 170 million women during their reproductive years alone.

metrics.name: Percentage of undiagnosed individuals
metrics.evidence_text: These factors delay diagnosis—with up to 70% of affected individuals remaining undiagnosed.

citations.title: PCOS gets a new name: Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome, to aid faster diagnosis, treatment
